# Restaurant Website (Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4)

Build a single-locale restaurant website with a full online menu (carta), 
WhatsApp order flow, gallery, contact/map, and about page. No CMS, no 
database, no i18n. Content lives in a single `content/es.json`.

**Reference build:** `references/tsuki-restaurante-build-session.md` — 
full Tsuki Restaurante Oriental site (Chinese-sushi fusion in San Lorenzo).

## Architecture

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router) |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) |
| Rendering | Static SSG (prerendered) |
| Deploy | Docker (`node:20-alpine` standalone) on Swarm behind Traefik |
| Content | JSON file (`content/es.json`) |
| Lead capture | WhatsApp deep link with pre-formatted message |
| Fonts | Inter (body) + Playfair Display (headings) |

### Stack Versions (May 2026)

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "lucide-react": "^0.510.0",
    "next": "^16.2.6",
    "react": "^19.2.6",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.6"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.2.4",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.2.4",
    "typescript": "^5.8.0"
  }
}
```

## Project Structure

```
tsuki-restaurante/
├── app/
│   ├── globals.css           # @theme colors, component classes
│   ├── layout.tsx            # Root layout: fonts, Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat
│   ├── page.tsx              # Homepage: Hero, Features, MenuPreview, Hours, CTA
│   ├── robots.ts             # SEO
│   ├── sitemap.ts            # SEO
│   ├── carta/
│   │   ├── page.tsx          # Server component (loads content, passes to client)
│   │   └── menu-client.tsx   # Client component: category nav, table/list, accordion
│   ├── nosotros/
│   │   └── page.tsx          # Server: story + values
│   ├── contacto/
│   │   └── page.tsx          # Server: info cards + Google Maps embed + WhatsApp CTA
│   └── galeria/
│       ├── page.tsx          # Server shell
│       └── gallery-client.tsx # Client: filter buttons + masonry grid + lightbox
├── components/
│   ├── header.tsx            # Fixed top nav, scroll-aware glass effect
│   ├── footer.tsx            # 3-column: brand, links, contact
│   ├── hero.tsx              # Full-screen hero with CTA buttons
│   ├── features-section.tsx  # 4-column icon grid (cocina china, sushi, wok, entradas)
│   ├── menu-preview-section.tsx # Category cards linking to /carta#category
│   ├── cta-banner.tsx        # Gradient CTA banner (used for WhatsApp)
│   └── whatsapp-float.tsx    # Fixed bottom-right WhatsApp button
├── content/
│   └── es.json               # ALL site content: site, nav, home, menu, about, gallery, contact
├── public/
│   └── images/               # Placeholder hero, gallery, og-image
├── next.config.mjs           # output: 'standalone'
├── postcss.config.mjs        # @tailwindcss/postcss
├── tsconfig.json
├── package.json
├── Dockerfile                # Two-stage build, node:20-alpine
└── docker-compose.yml        # Swarm + Traefik labels + healthcheck
```

## Content Structure (`content/es.json`)

The entire site is driven by a single JSON file with these sections:

| Section | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `site` | Name, phone, whatsapp, address, hours |
| `navigation` | Array of `{href, label}` for nav links |
| `home` | Hero content, features, menuSection, hours, cta |
| `menu` | Full menu data — categories, items, prices, notes |
| `about` | Story paragraphs, values array |
| `gallery` | Image array with src/alt/category |
| `contact` | Info cards, map embed, WhatsApp CTA |
| `footer` | Copyright, links, powered by |

### Menu Data Format

Two item types:

**List items** (most categories):
```json
{
  "id": "entradas",
  "name": "Entradas",
  "items": [
    { "name": "Arrolladitos de Primavera", "description": "Masa crocante...", "price": "Gs 20.000 (3 unid.) / Gs 30.000 (5 unid.)" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Apanado y frito a elección: +Gs 3.000"]
}
```

**Table items** (e.g. Salteados Wok — protein × format matrix):
```json
{
  "id": "salteados",
  "name": "Salteados Wok",
  "type": "table",
  "columns": ["Proteína", "Arroz Chino", "Fideo Arroz", "Chop Suey", "Yakisoba"],
  "rows": [
    ["Pollo", "Gs 37.000", "Gs 37.000", "Gs 35.000", "Gs 38.000"]
  ]
}
```

## Color Palette

Dark restaurant theme with gold/red accents:

```css
@theme {
  --color-primary: #8B0000;
  --color-primary-light: #CD5C5C;
  --color-accent: #D4AF37;
  --color-background: #0D0D0D;
  --color-surface: #1A1A1A;
  --color-surface-alt: #222222;
  --color-text: #F5F0E8;
  --color-text-muted: #888080;
}
```

## Key Client Components

### Menu Client (`carta/menu-client.tsx`)

This is the most complex component:

1. **Category navigation** — horizontal button bar (desktop only, `hidden md:flex`)
2. **Section-based layout** — each category is an `<section id="{id}">`
3. **Accordion on mobile** — category header buttons toggle content via `openCat` state
4. **URL hash navigation** — `useEffect` reads `window.location.hash` and scrolls to category
5. **Table rendering** — for wok/stir-fry categories, renders an HTML `<table>` with protein rows and format columns
6. **List rendering** — for all other categories, renders name+description+price cards
7. **Notes footer** — optional notes per category (e.g. "Apanado +3.000 Gs")

### Gallery Client (`galeria/gallery-client.tsx`)

- Category filter buttons (`todas`, `sushi`, `entradas`, `wok`)
- CSS columns-based masonry grid (`columns-2 md:columns-3`)
- Lightbox with full-screen overlay on click
- Placeholder SVG when no real images exist

### Header

- Fixes at top, becomes glass/blur on scroll via `useState(scrolled)` + `useEffect`
- Mobile hamburger menu with `useState(open)`
- Links from `content.json.navigation`

### WhatsApp Float

- Fixed bottom-right button, opens `https://wa.me/{number}`
- Centered in a component used across all pages

## Deployment: Docker Standalone Pitfalls

Next.js `output: 'standalone'` embeds the build path as `outputFileTracingRoot` 
in `server.js`. This causes failures when the Docker runtime path doesn't match.

### The Fix

Match `WORKDIR` in BOTH build and run stages:

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app          # ← this becomes outputFileTracingRoot
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci && npm run build

# Stage 2: Run — SAME WORKDIR as builder
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app          # ← MUST match builder's WORKDIR
# ... copy standalone output here
```

**Pitfall:** If WORKDIR differs between builder and runner, `server.js` contains 
`outputFileTracingRoot: "/some/host/path"` and the container will fail with 
`MODULE_NOT_FOUND` for server.js itself.

### Copy list for standalone

```dockerfile
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
```

## Deployment: Docker Swarm Healthcheck Pitfalls

### Problem 1: BusyBox wget doesn't support --spider

Alpine uses BusyBox wget, which lacks `--spider` and `--no-verbose`. 
Use this instead:

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
```

### Problem 2: Server binds to overlay IP, not 0.0.0.0

In Docker Swarm, Next.js standalone output with an `outputFileTracingRoot` set
may cause the server to bind to the overlay network IP (e.g. `10.0.2.110:3000`)
instead of `0.0.0.0`. This means `127.0.0.1:3000` and even `localhost:3000` may
be unreachable from inside the container.

**Fix 1 — Set HOSTNAME in the Dockerfile (required):**

```dockerfile
ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0
```

This forces Next.js to bind to all interfaces. Without this, the healthcheck
will fail repeatedly and Swarm will mark the container as unhealthy and kill it.

**Fix 2 — Use `localhost` in healthcheck (works with Fix 1 applied):**

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
```

Never use `127.0.0.1` in healthchecks — `localhost` resolves through the
container's `/etc/hosts` and works more reliably across network modes.

### Healthcheck config

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
  interval: 15s
  timeout: 5s
  retries: 3
  start_period: 5s
```

## Docker Compose for Swarm

```yaml
services:
  web:
    image: ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest
    ports: ["3000"]
    networks: [agent-net]
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.rule=Host(`tsuki.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.tsuki.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

## Image & Deploy Flow

```bash
# Build and tag
docker build -t tsuki:prod .
docker tag tsuki:prod ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest

# Deploy
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml tsuki

# Verify
docker service ps tsuki_web --no-trunc
curl -sI https://tsuki.paragu-ai.com/
```

## Add menu category client component

Create a client component (`app/carta/menu-client.tsx`) and pass menu categories 
as props from the server page. This is required because the menu page has 
interactive features (category navigation, accordion, hash scrolling) that need 
`"use client"`.

## HTML in layout.tsx

Due to Next.js 16 requirements, set `lang="es"` on `<html>` directly in the 
layout, and add font variable class names:

```tsx
<html lang="es" className={`${inter.variable} ${playfair.variable}`}>
```

## Pitfalls

- **WORKDIR mismatch between builder and runner**: Causes `server.js` to not 
  be found at runtime. Always use the same WORKDIR.
- **healthcheck with 127.0.0.1 fails in Swarm**: Server binds to overlay IP.
  Fix: set `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` in the Dockerfile AND use `localhost` in
  healthcheck commands, not `127.0.0.1`.
- **BusyBox wget lacks `--spider`**: Use `wget -q -O /dev/null http://localhost:3000/`
- **Container user can't read /root/**: Alpine's /root is 700 root-only. Use 
  `/app` or `/home/nextjs/app` as WORKDIR.
- **client component needs prop-based content**: Menu data from `content/es.json` 
  must be passed as props from server page to client component — you can't 
  import JSON directly in a `"use client"` file.
- **Gallery placeholder images**: If no real images exist yet, render a 
  placeholder SVG inside an aspect-square container rather than an empty div.
- **Next.js 15+ requires `let { children } = $props()` in layout**: This is 
  SvelteKit-specific, not an issue in Next.js. In Next.js 16, `children` 
  is received as a regular React prop.

# Restaurant Website (Next.js 16 App Router + Tailwind v4)

Build a single-locale restaurant website with a full online menu (carta), 
WhatsApp order flow, gallery, contact/map, and about page. No CMS, no 
database, no i18n. Content lives in a single `content/es.json`.

**Reference build:** `references/tsuki-restaurante-build-session.md` — 
full Tsuki Restaurante Oriental site (Chinese-sushi fusion in San Lorenzo).

## Architecture

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Framework | Next.js 16 (App Router) |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS v4 (`@tailwindcss/postcss`) |
| Rendering | Static SSG (prerendered) |
| Deploy | Docker (`node:20-alpine` standalone) on Swarm behind Traefik |
| Content | JSON file (`content/es.json`) |
| Lead capture | WhatsApp deep link with pre-formatted message |
| Fonts | Inter (body) + Playfair Display (headings) |

### Stack Versions (May 2026)

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "lucide-react": "^0.510.0",
    "next": "^16.2.6",
    "react": "^19.2.6",
    "react-dom": "^19.2.6"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.2.4",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.2.4",
    "typescript": "^5.8.0"
  }
}
```

## Project Structure

```
tsuki-restaurante/
├── app/
│   ├── globals.css           # @theme colors, component classes
│   ├── layout.tsx            # Root layout: fonts, Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat
│   ├── page.tsx              # Homepage: Hero, Features, MenuPreview, Hours, CTA
│   ├── robots.ts             # SEO
│   ├── sitemap.ts            # SEO
│   ├── carta/
│   │   ├── page.tsx          # Server component (loads content, passes to client)
│   │   └── menu-client.tsx   # Client component: category nav, table/list, accordion
│   ├── nosotros/
│   │   └── page.tsx          # Server: story + values
│   ├── contacto/
│   │   └── page.tsx          # Server: info cards + Google Maps embed + WhatsApp CTA
│   └── galeria/
│       ├── page.tsx          # Server shell
│       └── gallery-client.tsx # Client: filter buttons + masonry grid + lightbox
├── components/
│   ├── header.tsx            # Fixed top nav, scroll-aware glass effect
│   ├── footer.tsx            # 3-column: brand, links, contact
│   ├── hero.tsx              # Full-screen hero with CTA buttons
│   ├── features-section.tsx  # 4-column icon grid (cocina china, sushi, wok, entradas)
│   ├── menu-preview-section.tsx # Category cards linking to /carta#category
│   ├── cta-banner.tsx        # Gradient CTA banner (used for WhatsApp)
│   └── whatsapp-float.tsx    # Fixed bottom-right WhatsApp button
├── content/
│   └── es.json               # ALL site content: site, nav, home, menu, about, gallery, contact
├── public/
│   └── images/               # Placeholder hero, gallery, og-image
├── next.config.mjs           # output: 'standalone'
├── postcss.config.mjs        # @tailwindcss/postcss
├── tsconfig.json
├── package.json
├── Dockerfile                # Two-stage build, node:20-alpine
└── docker-compose.yml        # Swarm + Traefik labels + healthcheck
```

## Content Structure (`content/es.json`)

The entire site is driven by a single JSON file with these sections:

| Section | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `site` | Name, phone, whatsapp, address, hours |
| `navigation` | Array of `{href, label}` for nav links |
| `home` | Hero content, features, menuSection, hours, cta |
| `menu` | Full menu data — categories, items, prices, notes |
| `about` | Story paragraphs, values array |
| `gallery` | Image array with src/alt/category |
| `contact` | Info cards, map embed, WhatsApp CTA |
| `footer` | Copyright, links, powered by |

### Menu Data Format

Two item types:

**List items** (most categories):
```json
{
  "id": "entradas",
  "name": "Entradas",
  "items": [
    { "name": "Arrolladitos de Primavera", "description": "Masa crocante...", "price": "Gs 20.000 (3 unid.) / Gs 30.000 (5 unid.)" }
  ],
  "notes": ["Apanado y frito a elección: +Gs 3.000"]
}
```

**Table items** (e.g. Salteados Wok — protein × format matrix):
```json
{
  "id": "salteados",
  "name": "Salteados Wok",
  "type": "table",
  "columns": ["Proteína", "Arroz Chino", "Fideo Arroz", "Chop Suey", "Yakisoba"],
  "rows": [
    ["Pollo", "Gs 37.000", "Gs 37.000", "Gs 35.000", "Gs 38.000"]
  ]
}
```

## Color Palette

Dark restaurant theme with gold/red accents:

```css
@theme {
  --color-primary: #8B0000;
  --color-primary-light: #CD5C5C;
  --color-accent: #D4AF37;
  --color-background: #0D0D0D;
  --color-surface: #1A1A1A;
  --color-surface-alt: #222222;
  --color-text: #F5F0E8;
  --color-text-muted: #888080;
}
```

## Key Client Components

### Menu Client (`carta/menu-client.tsx`)

This is the most complex component:

1. **Category navigation** — horizontal button bar (desktop only, `hidden md:flex`)
2. **Section-based layout** — each category is an `<section id="{id}">`
3. **Accordion on mobile** — category header buttons toggle content via `openCat` state
4. **URL hash navigation** — `useEffect` reads `window.location.hash` and scrolls to category
5. **Table rendering** — for wok/stir-fry categories, renders an HTML `<table>` with protein rows and format columns
6. **List rendering** — for all other categories, renders name+description+price cards
7. **Notes footer** — optional notes per category (e.g. "Apanado +3.000 Gs")

### Gallery Client (`galeria/gallery-client.tsx`)

- Category filter buttons (`todas`, `sushi`, `entradas`, `wok`)
- CSS columns-based masonry grid (`columns-2 md:columns-3`)
- Lightbox with full-screen overlay on click
- Placeholder SVG when no real images exist

### Header

- Fixes at top, becomes glass/blur on scroll via `useState(scrolled)` + `useEffect`
- Mobile hamburger menu with `useState(open)`
- Links from `content.json.navigation`

### WhatsApp Float

- Fixed bottom-right button, opens `https://wa.me/{number}`
- Centered in a component used across all pages

## Deployment: Docker Standalone Pitfalls

Next.js `output: 'standalone'` embeds the build path as `outputFileTracingRoot` 
in `server.js`. This causes failures when the Docker runtime path doesn't match.

### The Fix

Match `WORKDIR` in BOTH build and run stages:

```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app          # ← this becomes outputFileTracingRoot
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci && npm run build

# Stage 2: Run — SAME WORKDIR as builder
FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app          # ← MUST match builder's WORKDIR
# ... copy standalone output here
```

**Pitfall:** If WORKDIR differs between builder and runner, `server.js` contains 
`outputFileTracingRoot: "/some/host/path"` and the container will fail with 
`MODULE_NOT_FOUND` for server.js itself.

### Copy list for standalone

```dockerfile
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
```

## Deployment: Docker Swarm Healthcheck Pitfalls

### Problem 1: BusyBox wget doesn't support --spider

Alpine uses BusyBox wget, which lacks `--spider` and `--no-verbose`. 
Use this instead:

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
```

### Problem 2: Server binds to overlay IP, not 0.0.0.0

In Docker Swarm, Next.js standalone output with an `outputFileTracingRoot` set
may cause the server to bind to the overlay network IP (e.g. `10.0.2.110:3000`)
instead of `0.0.0.0`. This means `127.0.0.1:3000` and even `localhost:3000` may
be unreachable from inside the container.

**Fix 1 — Set HOSTNAME in the Dockerfile (required):**

```dockerfile
ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0
```

This forces Next.js to bind to all interfaces. Without this, the healthcheck
will fail repeatedly and Swarm will mark the container as unhealthy and kill it.

**Fix 2 — Use `localhost` in healthcheck (works with Fix 1 applied):**

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
```

Never use `127.0.0.1` in healthchecks — `localhost` resolves through the
container's `/etc/hosts` and works more reliably across network modes.

### Healthcheck config

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
  interval: 15s
  timeout: 5s
  retries: 3
  start_period: 5s
```

## Docker Compose for Swarm

```yaml
services:
  web:
    image: ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest
    ports: ["3000"]
    networks: [agent-net]
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 2
      labels:
        - "traefik.enable=true"
        - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.entrypoints=websecure"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.rule=Host(`tsuki.paragu-ai.com`)"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.tls=true"
        - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver"
        - "traefik.http.services.tsuki.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"]
      interval: 15s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

networks:
  agent-net:
    external: true
```

## Image & Deploy Flow

```bash
# Build and tag
docker build -t tsuki:prod .
docker tag tsuki:prod ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest
docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest

# Deploy
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml tsuki

# Verify
docker service ps tsuki_web --no-trunc
curl -sI https://tsuki.paragu-ai.com/
```

## Add menu category client component

Create a client component (`app/carta/menu-client.tsx`) and pass menu categories 
as props from the server page. This is required because the menu page has 
interactive features (category navigation, accordion, hash scrolling) that need 
`"use client"`.

## HTML in layout.tsx

Due to Next.js 16 requirements, set `lang="es"` on `<html>` directly in the 
layout, and add font variable class names:

```tsx
<html lang="es" className={`${inter.variable} ${playfair.variable}`}>
```

## Pitfalls

- **WORKDIR mismatch between builder and runner**: Causes `server.js` to not 
  be found at runtime. Always use the same WORKDIR.
- **healthcheck with 127.0.0.1 fails in Swarm**: Server binds to overlay IP.
  Fix: set `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` in the Dockerfile AND use `localhost` in
  healthcheck commands, not `127.0.0.1`.
- **BusyBox wget lacks `--spider`**: Use `wget -q -O /dev/null http://localhost:3000/`
- **Container user can't read /root/**: Alpine's /root is 700 root-only. Use 
  `/app` or `/home/nextjs/app` as WORKDIR.
- **client component needs prop-based content**: Menu data from `content/es.json` 
  must be passed as props from server page to client component — you can't 
  import JSON directly in a `"use client"` file.
- **Gallery placeholder images**: If no real images exist yet, render a 
  placeholder SVG inside an aspect-square container rather than an empty div.
- **Next.js 15+ requires `let { children } = $props()` in layout**: This is 
  SvelteKit-specific, not an issue in Next.js. In Next.js 16, `children` 
  is received as a regular React prop.

Image Sourcing from Unsplash For a restaurant site, source free high-quality food images via direct Unsplash download URLs. Use `curl -sL -o ''` for each image. ### Unsplash direct download URL pattern ``` https://images.unsplash.com/photo-?w=&q=80 ``` No API key needed. The `w` param controls width. ### Good search queries for restaurant images - Chinese/interior: `Chinese restaurant interior dim sum` - Sushi: `sushi platter close up`, `japanese sushi rolls` - Wok/stir-fry: `chinese food stir fry wok`, `noodles chopsticks asian food` - Appetizers: `spring rolls vietnamese appetizer`, `gyoza dumplings japanese` - Seafood: `fried shrimp seafood asian` - Restaurant interior: `restaurant interior cozy` ### Download all images ```python food_images = { "hero-bg.jpg": {"url": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[REDACTED_PHONE]f8b828add9?w=1600&q=80"}, "sushi-1.jpg": {"url": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[REDACTED_PHONE]cf80d66c?w=800&q=80"}, "wok-1.jpg": {"url": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-[REDACTED_PHONE]be2db19?w=800&q=80"}, } for filename, info in food_images.items(): terminal(f"curl -sL -o public/images/{filename} '{info['url']}'") ``` ### Files to generate Place images in `public/images/`: - `hero-bg.jpg` — 1600px wide for hero background - `sushi-1.jpg`, `sushi-2.jpg`, `wok-1.jpg`, `wok-2.jpg`, `spring-rolls.jpg`, `gyozas.jpg`, `shrimp.jpg`, `restaurant-interior.jpg` — 800px for gallery/features Update `content/es.json` to reference these paths in both `gallery.images[]` and `home.hero.bgImage`. ## Order Page (`/pedidos`) — Interactive Cart → WhatsApp The order page is a client component (`"use client"`) with full cart state management. It reuses the same menu `categories` data structure as the carta page, but each item has an "add to cart" button instead of just displaying. ### Architecture ``` Server page (pedidos/page.tsx) → loads content/es.json (menu.categories, site.whatsapp, order.cta) → passes data as props to client component → OrderPageClient (pedidos/order-client.tsx) → STATE: cart (CartItem[]), activeCat, openCat, showCart, name, sent → Left: menu + "+" buttons per item → Right/overlay: cart sidebar with qty controls + send button ``` ### Cart Data Model ```typescript interface CartItem { id: string; // `${name}::${price}` — dedup key name: string; qty: number; price: string; // "Gs 20.000" — original price string } ``` ### Cart Logic ```typescript const [cart, setCart] = useState([]); // Add item (increment if exists, else push) function addItem(name: string, price: string) { const id = `${name}::${price}`; setCart((prev) => { const existing = prev.find((i) => i.id === id); if (existing) return prev.map((i) => i.id === id ? {...i, qty: i.qty + 1} : i); return [...prev, {id, name, qty: 1, price}]; }); } // Update quantity or remove at 0 function updateQty(id: string, delta: number) { setCart((prev) => prev.map((i) => i.id === id ? {...i, qty: Math.max(0, i.qty + delta)} : i).filter((i) => i.qty > 0)); } // Auto-calculate total const cartTotal = useMemo(() => cart.reduce((sum, item) => sum + parsePrice(item.price) * item.qty, 0), [cart]); ``` ### WhatsApp Message Format The message opens WhatsApp with a pre-formatted order: ``` 🧾 Pedido Tsuki Restaurante Oriental • Arrolladitos de Primavera x2 — Gs 20.000 • New York Roll x1 — Gs 27.000 • Sushi Mix 25 piezas x1 — Gs 90.000 Total: Gs 137.000 Nombre: Juan Pérez ``` ### Send Logic ```typescript function buildWhatsAppMessage(): string { if (cart.length === 0) return ""; const lines = ["*🧾 Pedido Tsuki Restaurante Oriental*", ""]; cart.forEach((item) => lines.push(`• ${item.name} x${item.qty} — ${item.price}`)); lines.push("", `*Total: Gs ${cartTotal.toLocaleString("es-PY")}*`); if (name.trim()) lines.push("", `*Nombre:* ${name.trim()}`); return encodeURIComponent(lines.join("\n")); } function handleSend() { window.open(`https://wa.me/${whatsapp}?text=${msg}`, "_blank"); } ``` ### UI Layout | Viewport | Layout | |----------|--------| | Desktop (lg+) | Two-column: menu on left (flex-1) + sticky cart sidebar (w-80) | | Mobile | Menu full-width + FAB cart button (fixed bottom-20) + bottom sheet drawer | ### Cart Features - **Per-item qty controls**: +/- buttons, remove (trash icon) - **Item dedup**: Same item+price = increment qty, not duplicate row - **Auto total**: `useMemo` recalculates when cart changes - **Name field**: Optional text input included in the message - **Send button**: Opens WhatsApp with pre-formatted message - **Clear all**: Single button to empty cart - **Confirmation state**: Button shows checkmark briefly after send - **Desktop sidebar**: Sticky, always visible - **Mobile FAB**: Floating action button with count badge → bottom sheet drawer ### Order Page CTA Content (in content/es.json) ```json "order": { "seo": { "title": "Pedidos — Tsuki Restaurante Oriental", "description": "..." }, "hero": { "title": "Hacé tu pedido", "subtitle": "..." }, "categories": [{ "id": "entradas", "name": "Entradas" }, ...], "cta": { "title": "Confirmá tu pedido", "subtitle": "...", "buttonText": "Enviar pedido por WhatsApp", "emptyText": "Tu carrito está vacío — agregá productos de la carta" } } ``` ## Key Client Components ### Menu Client (`carta/menu-client.tsx`) The most complex component: 1. **Category navigation** — horizontal button bar (desktop only, `hidden md:flex`) 2. **Section-based layout** — each category is an `
` 3. **Accordion on mobile** — category header buttons toggle content via `openCat` state 4. **URL hash navigation** — `useEffect` reads `window.location.hash` and scrolls to category 5. **Table rendering** — for wok/stir-fry categories, renders an HTML `` with protein rows and format columns 6. **List rendering** — for all other categories, renders name+description+price cards 7. **Notes footer** — optional notes per category (e.g. "Apanado +3.000 Gs") ### Gallery Client (`galeria/gallery-client.tsx`) - Category filter buttons (todas, sushi, entradas, wok, mariscos, local) - CSS columns-based masonry grid (`columns-2 md:columns-3 gap-4`) - Lightbox with full-screen overlay + caption on click - Images rendered as `` with `loading="lazy"` and hover zoom effect ### Header - Fixes at top, becomes glass/blur on scroll via `useState(scrolled)` + `useEffect` - Mobile hamburger menu with `useState(open)` - Links from `content.json.navigation` ### WhatsApp Float - Fixed bottom-right button, opens `https://wa.me/{number}` - Centered in a component used across all pages ## Deployment: Docker Standalone Pitfalls Next.js `output: 'standalone'` embeds the build path as `outputFileTracingRoot` in `server.js`. This causes failures when the Docker runtime path doesn't match. ### The Fix Match `WORKDIR` in BOTH build and run stages: ```dockerfile FROM node:20-alpine AS builder WORKDIR /app # ← becomes outputFileTracingRoot RUN npm ci && npm run build FROM node:20-alpine AS runner WORKDIR /app # ← MUST match builder's WORKDIR COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./ COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content ``` **Pitfall:** If WORKDIR differs between builder and runner, `server.js` contains `outputFileTracingRoot: "/some/host/path"` and the container will fail with `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` for server.js itself. ## Deployment: Docker Swarm Healthcheck Pitfalls ### Problem 1: BusyBox wget doesn't support --spider Alpine uses BusyBox wget, which lacks `--spider` and `--no-verbose`. **Fix:** ```yaml healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"] ``` ### Problem 2: Server binds to overlay IP, not 0.0.0.0 In Docker Swarm, Next.js standalone output with an `outputFileTracingRoot` set may cause the server to bind to the overlay network IP (e.g. `10.0.2.110:3000`) instead of `0.0.0.0`. This means `127.0.0.1:3000` and even `localhost:3000` may be unreachable from inside the container. **Fix 1 — Set HOSTNAME in the Dockerfile (required):** ```dockerfile ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 ``` This forces Next.js to bind to all interfaces. Without this, the healthcheck will fail repeatedly and Swarm will mark the container as unhealthy and kill it. **Fix 2 — Use `localhost` in healthcheck (works with Fix 1 applied):** ```yaml healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"] ``` Never use `127.0.0.1` in healthchecks — `localhost` resolves through the container's `/etc/hosts` and works more reliably across network modes. ### Healthcheck config ```yaml healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"] interval: 15s timeout: 5s retries: 3 start_period: 5s ``` ## Docker Compose for Swarm ```yaml services: web: image: ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest ports: ["3000"] networks: [agent-net] deploy: mode: replicated replicas: 2 labels: - "traefik.enable=true" - "traefik.docker.network=agent-net" - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.entrypoints=websecure" - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.rule=Host(`tsuki.paragu-ai.com`)" - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.tls=true" - "traefik.http.routers.tsuki.tls.certresolver=letsencryptresolver" - "traefik.http.services.tsuki.loadbalancer.server.port=3000" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "-O", "/dev/null", "http://localhost:3000/"] interval: 15s timeout: 5s retries: 3 start_period: 5s networks: agent-net: external: true ``` ## Image & Deploy Flow ```bash # Build and tag docker build -t tsuki:prod . docker tag tsuki:prod ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest docker push ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest # Deploy (first time) docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml tsuki # Update (subsequent) docker service update --image ghcr.io/ai-whisperers/tsuki:latest --force tsuki_web # Verify docker service ps tsuki_web --no-trunc curl -sI https://tsuki.paragu-ai.com/ ``` ## Client component data flow Create a client component (e.g. `app/carta/menu-client.tsx` or `app/pedidos/order-client.tsx`) and pass menu categories + whatsapp number as props from the server page. This is required because interactive features (cart, category navigation, accordion) need `"use client"`. ```tsx // Server page import content from "@/content/es.json"; import MenuPageClient from "./menu-client"; export default function Page() { return ; } ``` ## HTML in layout.tsx ```tsx ``` ## Pitfalls - **WORKDIR mismatch between builder and runner**: Causes `server.js` to not be found at runtime. Always use the same WORKDIR. - **healthcheck with 127.0.0.1 fails in Swarm**: Server binds to overlay IP. Fix: set `ENV HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0` in the Dockerfile AND use `localhost` in healthcheck commands, not `127.0.0.1`. - **BusyBox wget lacks `--spider`**: Use `wget -q -O /dev/null http://localhost:3000/` - **Container user can't read /root/**: Alpine's /root is 700 root-only. Use `/app` or `/home/nextjs/app` as WORKDIR. - **client component needs prop-based content**: Menu data from `content/es.json` must be passed as props from server page to client component — you can't import JSON directly in a `"use client"` file. - **Cart dedup key**: Use `${name}::${price}` as the dedup key so the same item at different price tiers (e.g. 5pz vs 10pz) are separate cart entries. - **Unsplash images have no guaranteed permanence**: The direct URLs may change or break over time. Always commit the actual downloaded files to the repo. - **Gallery placeholder images**: If no real images exist yet, render a placeholder SVG inside an aspect-square container rather than an empty div. - **Open WhatsApp on mobile**: `window.open("https://wa.me/{number}?text={msg}", "_blank")` works on both desktop (opens web) and mobile (opens app). ## Verification Checklist - [ ] `npm run build` succeeds with all routes showing `○ (Static)` - [ ] All pages return 200 when tested via curl on localhost:3000 - [ ] Docker container starts and responds: `docker run --rm -p 3000:3000 tsuki:prod` - [ ] Swarm healthcheck shows `(healthy)` not `(starting)` - [ ] Traefik routing: `curl -sI https://tsuki.paragu-ai.com/` → 200 - [ ] Menu category navigation scrolls to correct section (`window.location.hash`) - [ ] Order cart: add items, adjust quantities, see total update - [ ] Order send: WhatsApp opens with formatted message including all items + total + name - [ ] Order mobile: FAB shows correct count, bottom sheet opens with cart - [ ] Gallery: images render, filters work, lightbox opens on click - [ ] WhatsApp buttons open correct number - [ ] Map embed renders (test with browser, not curl) - [ ] Cloudflare DNS: A record → VPS IP, proxy on ", "name": "restaurant-website-nextjs"}
